You are ARIA — Agentic Risk Intelligence for AML — rule performance specialist. You analyze AML monitoring rules using SAR backtesting and 2D parameter sweeps. IMPORTANT: You MUST respond entirely in English.

RULES — follow these exactly:
1. For SAR backtest questions about a specific rule: call rule_sar_backtest directly.
2. For 2D sweep questions about a specific rule: call rule_2d_sweep directly.
3. Do NOT call list_rules when the user asks about a specific rule — call the analysis tool.
4. After receiving tool results, copy the PRE-COMPUTED section verbatim.
5. Add ONE sentence of AML insight using ONLY numbers from the tool result.
6. Do NOT invent rule names, TP/FP counts, or thresholds not in the tool result.
7. Call the tool ONCE only.
8. When the user asks about a specific cluster, pass cluster as an integer.
9. If list_rules shows alerts=0 for a rule, state it has no production data.
10. The system contains exactly 11 active AML rules.
What is the SAR filing rate for Elder Abuse rule?

Tool result for rule_sar_backtest:
=== PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Elder Abuse
Current condition: Age >= 60 AND 14-day outgoing >= $5K AND >= 3 std dev above 90-day mean
Sweep parameter: z_threshold - Std-dev multiplier above 90-day mean (currently 3)
Current value: 3.0
Labeled population: 1,146 customers (TP+FN pool=188 SAR, FP+TN pool=958 non-SAR, precision=16.4%)

At the lowest value (0.00): TP=188, FP=958, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%, precision=16.4%).
At current condition (3.00): TP=188, FP=958, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%, precision=16.4%).
To keep TP rate >=90%: z_threshold <= 5.00 => TP=172, FP=710, FN=16, TN=248, precision=19.5%.
To keep TP rate >=50%: z_threshold <= 9.00 => TP=101, FP=298, FN=87, TN=660, precision=25.3%.
At the highest value (12.00): TP=64, FP=182, FN=124, TN=776 (TP rate=34.0%, precision=26.0%).
(Detailed sweep table shown in the chart below.)
=== END PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST ===

=== PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Elder Abuse
Sweep parameter: z_threshold (currently 3)
Labeled population: 1,146 customers (SAR=188, FP=958, precision=16.4%)

At current condition (z_threshold=3.00): TP=188, FP=958, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%).
To keep TP rate >=90%: z_threshold <= 5.00 => TP=172, FP=710, FN=16, TN=248, precision=19.5%.
To keep TP rate >=50%: z_threshold <= 9.00 => TP=101, FP=298, FN=87, TN=660, precision=25.3%.
At highest value (12.00): TP=64, FP=182, FN=124, TN=776 (TP rate=34.0%, precision=26.0%).

The Elder Abuse rule catches 100% of the 188 SAR customers at the current z_threshold of 3, but raising to 5 reduces false positives by 26% (958 to 710) while retaining 91.5% of SARs.

Run SAR backtest for Activity Deviation ACH rule

Tool result for rule_sar_backtest:
=== PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Activity Deviation (ACH)
Current condition: Monthly Outgoing ACH >= $50K AND >= 5 std dev above 12-month profile mean
Sweep parameter: floor_amount - Minimum monthly Outgoing ACH sum to trigger (currently $50K)
Current value: 50000
Labeled population: 487 customers (TP+FN pool=82 SAR, FP+TN pool=405 non-SAR, precision=16.8%)

At the lowest value (10000.00): TP=82, FP=405, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%, precision=16.8%).
At current condition (50000.00): TP=82, FP=405, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%, precision=16.8%).
To keep TP rate >=90%: floor_amount <= 75000.00 => TP=74, FP=288, FN=8, TN=117, precision=20.4%.
To keep TP rate >=50%: floor_amount <= 150000.00 => TP=44, FP=118, FN=38, TN=287, precision=27.2%.
At the highest value (200000.00): TP=28, FP=62, FN=54, TN=343 (TP rate=34.1%, precision=31.1%).
(Detailed sweep table shown in the chart below.)
=== END PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST ===

=== PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Activity Deviation (ACH)
Sweep parameter: floor_amount (currently $50K)
Labeled population: 487 customers (SAR=82, FP=405, precision=16.8%)

At current condition (50000): TP=82, FP=405, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%, precision=16.8%).
To keep TP rate >=90%: floor_amount <= 75000 => TP=74, FP=288, FN=8, TN=117, precision=20.4%.
To keep TP rate >=50%: floor_amount <= 150000 => TP=44, FP=118, FN=38, TN=287, precision=27.2%.
At highest value (200000): TP=28, FP=62, FN=54, TN=343 (TP rate=34.1%, precision=31.1%).

Raising the ACH floor to $75K retains 90.2% of the 82 SAR customers while cutting false positives by 29% (405 to 288).

Show 2D analysis for Detect Excessive Transaction Activity

Tool result for rule_2d_sweep:
=== PRE-COMPUTED 2D SWEEP (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Detect Excessive Transaction Activity
Axis 1 (floor_amount): Minimum N-day incoming Cash+Check sum to trigger (currently $10K over 5 days)
Axis 2 (time_window): Aggregation window in days (currently 5 days)
Grid: 9 x 5 = 45 combinations
SAR pool: 46  Non-SAR pool: 310

At current condition (floor_amount=10000, time_window=5): TP=40, FP=268, FN=6, TN=42 (TP rate=87.0%).
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=90%): floor_amount=8000, time_window=3 => TP=42, FP=240, FN=4, TN=70, TP rate=91.3%, precision=14.9%.
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=50%): floor_amount=25000, time_window=7 => TP=24, FP=104, FN=22, TN=206, TP rate=52.2%, precision=18.8%.
(Heatmap shown in the chart below.)
=== END PRE-COMPUTED 2D SWEEP ===

=== PRE-COMPUTED 2D SWEEP (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Detect Excessive Transaction Activity
Axis 1 (floor_amount): Minimum N-day incoming Cash+Check sum (currently $10K over 5 days)
Axis 2 (time_window): Aggregation window in days (currently 5 days)
Grid: 9 x 5 = 45 combinations | SAR pool: 46 | Non-SAR pool: 310

At current (floor_amount=10000, time_window=5): TP=40, FP=268, FN=6, TN=42 (TP rate=87.0%).
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=90%): floor_amount=8000, time_window=3 => TP=42, FP=240, FN=4, TN=70, precision=14.9%.
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=50%): floor_amount=25000, time_window=7 => TP=24, FP=104, FN=22, TN=206, precision=18.8%.

Shortening the window to 3 days and lowering the floor to $8K actually improves the TP rate to 91.3% while cutting FPs by 10% (268 to 240).

Show 2D heatmap for Velocity Single — how do floor_amount and z_threshold interact?

Tool result for rule_2d_sweep:
=== PRE-COMPUTED 2D SWEEP (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Velocity Single
Axis 1 (pair_total): Minimum combined in+out pair total to trigger (currently $20K)
Axis 2 (ratio_tolerance): Max deviation of out/in ratio from 1.0 to trigger (currently 10%)
Grid: 9 x 9 = 81 combinations
SAR pool: 74  Non-SAR pool: 404

At current condition (pair_total=20000, ratio_tolerance=0.10): TP=74, FP=404, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%).
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=90%): pair_total=30000, ratio_tolerance=0.075 => TP=68, FP=298, FN=6, TN=106, TP rate=91.9%, precision=18.6%.
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=50%): pair_total=60000, ratio_tolerance=0.05 => TP=38, FP=112, FN=36, TN=292, TP rate=51.4%, precision=25.3%.
(Heatmap shown in the chart below.)
=== END PRE-COMPUTED 2D SWEEP ===

=== PRE-COMPUTED 2D SWEEP (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Rule: Velocity Single
Axis 1 (pair_total): Minimum combined in+out pair total (currently $20K)
Axis 2 (ratio_tolerance): Max out/in ratio deviation from 1.0 (currently 10%)
Grid: 9 x 9 = 81 combinations | SAR pool: 74 | Non-SAR pool: 404

Note: Velocity Single uses pair_total and ratio_tolerance — not floor_amount or z_threshold. The sweep was run on the actual parameters.

At current (pair_total=20000, ratio_tolerance=0.10): TP=74, FP=404, FN=0, TN=0 (TP rate=100.0%).
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=90%): pair_total=30000, ratio_tolerance=0.075 => TP=68, FP=298, FN=6, TN=106, precision=18.6%.
Best FP reduction (TP rate >=50%): pair_total=60000, ratio_tolerance=0.05 => TP=38, FP=112, FN=36, TN=292, precision=25.3%.

Tightening both the pair_total to $30K and the ratio tolerance to 7.5% retains 91.9% of SARs while reducing false positives by 26% (404 to 298).

=== PRE-COMPUTED THRESHOLD ANALYSIS (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
